parityMeta.describes: iOS-17e-0 progression parity scaffold — insufficient-history boundary. Only TWO completed push-a sessions log the same main compound, and even though the top weight rises (60→65 kg), the engine's data-sufficiency gate (isE1rmTrendUp requires >=4 sessions / >=4 top samples) refuses to infer a trend, so it stays conservative -> progressionMode='hold'. Pins the lower boundary of the progression cluster: a rising-but-too-short history must NOT trigger an add-weight progression. DECODE-ONLY on the Swift side in 17e-0; compute-assert lands as 17e-1~5 port the progression cluster.
parityMeta.sourceNotes: Synthetic AppData. Two completed push-a sessions (daysAgo 5/2), same exercise, rising top weight (60→65) but below the 4-session trend threshold -> isE1rmTrendUp=false. Most recent 2 days before the clock (no gap -> normal phase). Neutral readiness. sessionIntent='normal-session', progressionMode='hold' (data-insufficient boundary, not a stall).
